title: Western Oregon to San Mateo route detour check
source: Sierra travel-routing incident on 2026-06-09; Tango jobs manual test checklist travel coverage
system: You are Sierra, a careful travel-planning assistant. Use tools when a factual route, drive-time, or detour claim depends on real map data. Do not answer route questions from mental geography when routing tools are available. Label tool-backed facts separately from synthesis.
prompt: I'm driving from Yachats, Oregon to San Mateo, California. If I leave after picking up a child from school around 2:00 p.m., where would it make sense to stop around 8 or 9 p.m.? Also, is Sacramento actually on the fastest route, or would routing through Sacramento add driving time compared with staying on I-5/CA-505 toward the Bay Area? Please compare the direct/best option against a Sacramento waypoint before recommending a stop corridor.
successCriteria: Calls osrm_route before making a route, ETA, detour, or stop-corridor conclusion.
Compares at least one direct/best route against one Sacramento-waypoint route.
Does not claim Sacramento is on the fastest route unless OSRM output supports that claim.
Distinguishes OSRM-backed route facts from lodging/preference synthesis.
Recommends an overnight-stop corridor with uncertainty if exact hotel selection is not checked.
knownFailureModes: Answers from mental geography without osrm_route.
Treats Sacramento as a natural route to San Mateo without comparing CA-505 / Vacaville style options.
Says it verified the geography but makes no tool call.
Overstates exact arrival times without accounting for stops or uncertainty.

rubric.name: tool_grounding
rubric.description: Required route conclusions are backed by osrm_route output.

rubric.name: route_reasoning
rubric.description: Correctly compares direct versus Sacramento-route tradeoffs and avoids misleading geography.

rubric.name: user_utility
rubric.description: Gives a practical stop corridor and next-step recommendation.

rubric.name: honesty
rubric.description: Clearly separates verified facts, estimates, and synthesis.
